title: Self-Conscious Emotions and the Right Fronto-Temporal and Right Temporal Parietal Junction.

assets.abstract: For more than two decades, research focusing on both clinical and non-clinical populations has suggested a key role for specific regions in the regulation of self-conscious emotions. It is speculated that both the expression and the interpretation of self-conscious emotions are critical in humans for action planning and response, communication, learning, parenting, and most social encounters. Empathy, Guilt, Jealousy, Shame, and Pride are all categorized as self-conscious emotions, all of which

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=7.5: links Wind/Pride concept to mechanistic neuroscience
